full_text: [SLIDE 1]: GenAI Overview [SLIDE 2]: Project 1: Report Composer Overview: Augmented physician workflow tool to support filling in medical reports. Reduces time needed for physicians to work through disparate data sets across EHR, diagnostics, and various documents. Fills in as many fields as possible; still requires physicians review, and to fill in responses the tool could not generate. Approach: Developed a full-stack Generative AI application using low-code/no-code platform for UI, Google Cloud Platform and Tools, and Google’s domain specific LLM – MedLM / MedPaLM2. Value: Reduced physician burden by reducing time to complete reports, improved quality measured by the reduced number of rejected reports, and reduced QA costs. eTrace Approval ID# 24-leidos-1105-28518 [SLIDE 3]: Project 2: Agentic Exploratory Data Analysis Overview: The first of a 2-phase project that aids in analyzing data sets and generates reports that include details on features and characteristics of data; relationships between and patterns in the data; duplicates, missing values, anomalies, outliers, scale; and multivariate analysis. Approach: Uses LLM agents to dynamically plan, task, generate and execute code against any data set at its source location in minutes, providing deep insights into the data. LLM Agent framework: Autogen; LLMs: Claude 3.5 Sonnet and Azure OpenAI GPT-4; AI Concepts: LLM Agents, Generative AI, Hierarchical Task Networks and Behavioral Trees Value: Adaptable planning and tasking that enables analysis of structured and unstructured data, leading to a faster insights with an accurate and complete data picture. eTrace Approval ID# 24-leidos-1105-28518 [SLIDE 4]: Project 3: Communication Assistant Overview: Communication AI assistant solution supporting a time-sensitive collaborative process between Providers and Leidos QTC QA specialists. This tool aims to reduce miscommunication, increase workflow efficiency, improve quality and improve user satisfaction. Approach: A lightweight tool that leverages secure Generative AI model, GPT-4, to help QA specialists communicate effectively and efficiently by enhancing the language used in their questions to Providers. Value: Reduced miscommunication, increased workflow efficiency, and improved quality and improve user satisfaction. Testing yielded an 80% agreement between the Generative AI model’s answers and end users. eTrace Approval ID# 24-leidos-1105-28518 [SLIDE 5]: Project 4: Modular Container-Based Q&A Overview: Secure, localized, compartmentalized Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) application that can easily be adapted for deployment in both restricted and open systems, and is environment independent. Approach: Leverage Large Language Models (LLMS), prompt engineering and hybrid search while providing transparency to the underlying source of information used to generate the answers, all within a containerized framework. Value: Easily adaptable and self-contained in various environments; adaptable security; transparent answer generation; internal logic aligns with human workflows; role-based access controls restrict information to people with correct credentials. eTrace Approval ID# 24-leidos-1105-28518 [SLIDE 6]: Project 5: Technical Language Simplification Overview: A marketing tool developed to perform language simplification by summarizing a source article and simplifying its technical language. It generates a first draft of the potential summarization based on particular desirable stylistics. Approach: Uses Azure OpenAI’s GPT-4, combined with prompt engineering, to generate a summary of complex information into a more accessible voice and in the proper length based on initial user parameters. Value: Enables non-technical teams to effectively engage with a range of technological topics by distilling the information quickly and presenting summaries for non-expert audiences without losing the substance of the technological information. eTrace Approval ID# 24-leidos-1105-28518 [SLIDE 7]: Health & Civil Sector ManagedX Intelligent Document Processing (IDP) [SLIDE 8]: 8 Leidos supports customers across federal agencies and the DoD that have a common problem: Processing and dispositioning millions of documents every year. Leidos has invested in a Strategic Collaboration Agreement (SCA) with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to create a repeatable, cloud-based offering called ManagedX IDP that automates document processing workflows through its ability to extract and process data, apply AI models, and provide visualization. Unlike other intelligent document processing solutions on the market, ManagedX IDP is tailored to your business domain and implemented according to the unique needs of your workflow. Document Processing: A Pervasive Problem Leidos + AWS Market Differentiation INTRO & BACKGROUND Leidos Domain Expertise + AWS Tech Innovation = Differentiated Solution [SLIDE 9]: 9 Agencies are overwhelmed by large amounts of documents, applications, and other data submissions that over-burden their people, processes and systems every day. Information Processing Overload Today, processing requires manual, laborious workflows which are highly inefficient and prone to error. Inefficient Manual Processes The current state of document processing technology is insufficient at meeting the growing demand Demand Outpacing Capacity ThE PROBLEM: INEFFICIENT DOCUMENT PROCESSING Manual document workflows are a major inefficiency in organizations [SLIDE 10]: 10 10 BEFORE MANAGEDX IDP AFTER MANAGEDX IDP Cuts the average time per document in half—tripling the efficiency without adding staff Delivering 3X efficiency gains to the mission [SLIDE 11]: 11 OTHER POTENTIAL USE CASES Social Security Litigation Federal Grant Applications In the event of Social Security fraud, ManagedX IDP could be applied to the E-Discovery process of compiling and organizing evidence. In the world or Government Grants applications, ManagedX IDP can condense all of the documentation to assist in the review process. Broad applicability and flexibility [SLIDE 12]: 12 Managedx Architecture [SLIDE 13]: 13 DOMAIN EXPERTISE & AUTOMATED WORKFLOWS ManagedXis a solution tailored to your business domain and implemented according to the unique needs of your workflow. MANAGEDX IDP: Solution Breakdown Modular design allows us to meet you where you are [SLIDE 14]: MANAGEDXis a solution tailored to your businessdomain and implemented according to theunique needs of your workflow.​ ENGAGEMENT MODEL: An Incremental Path to Value Target 90 days to value [SLIDE 15]: 15 15 Summary Leidos Domain Expertise + AWS Tech Innovation = Differentiated Solution Manual document workflows are a major bottleneck in organizations Delivering value to the Mission Broad applicability and flexibility Modular design allows us to meet you where you are Target 90 days to value [SLIDE 16]: Insert your Leidos presentation title here 16 Using Gen AI Large Language Models to Transform Training [SLIDE 17]: External Technology Partnerships 17 Using Generative AI Large Language Models (LLMs) For Embedded Help and Training [SLIDE 18]: 18 Apply GenAI LLM to provide an embedded Help/Training capability Description: Traditional methods of incorporating embedded help and embedded training (EH/ET) into systems or software applications have typically involved the creation of one or more of the following training and performance support products: Interactive Multimedia Instruction (IMI), user manuals, tutorials, and digital job aids. These traditional methods are essential, and the content still has to be produced. However, aside from time-consuming efforts to produce this material, use of the material often forces users to search through volumes of the content in order to find the specific information they need at the time of use. Furthermore, as any system evolves over time, updating system system capabilities into training/help content and maintaining physical products becomes a significant ongoing task. Recommendation: Deploy an Embedded Help and Training capability utilizing a Generative Artificial Intelligence Large Language Model (Gen AI LLM) using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)-based AI. The capability would include natural spoken language (in multiple languages), as well as written, interfaces. [SLIDE 19]: 19 Apply GenAI LLM to provide an embedded Help/Training capability (cont) Way(s) to Address the Recommendation: Leverage and apply the Leidos IRaD effort on domain-specific EH/ET that uses Gen AI LLMs. The results have been shown to significantly improve user performance on any system, and can be easily applied to a Ground Combat Vehicle for instance Benefits: Provides timely, accurate information to users on-demand at the point of need, 24/7/365. Significantly improves timeliness, accuracy, and efficiency of information retrieval for end-users. Provides efficient and effective task training and performance support with significantly reduced resources. Supports enhanced decision-making, while providing performance support at the point of need to improve job performance. [SLIDE 20]: 20 Key Points Use COTs Tools such as Microsoft Azure Info Assistant The Technology was developed and tested in FY23 in the Leidos AI Accelerator (Horizon 2025 and Project Socrates). Info Assist currently has the best (practical) UI and data management for user defined data sets for operators not requiring IT and computer software skills. (e.g. Soldiers.) Recently received FEDRAMP Approval. DOD IL 4-5 approval imminent Use of Curated, Authoritative Source Datasets Only e.g. Technical Publications, Critical Tasks, Doctrine ensures accuracy and instills trust [SLIDE 21]: Insert your Leidos Demonstration Transform Training Corey Hendricks, 8/18/2023 [SLIDE 25]: 25 Leidos IRAD seeks to understand the impact of Generative AI (LLMs) to Transform Training Socrates, a Leidos IRAD project, seeks to build custom query capability that utilizes two curated datasets based on: Army Doctrinal Publications: Distribution A, Approved for Public Release Socrates Objective: Construct system capable of swiftly and precisely retrieving accurate data from a large volume of Army doctrinal pubs Advanced Natural Language processing techniques and Machine Learning algorithms will be incorporated to create queries based on User input and contextual information gleaned from curated data Output will be intuitively formatted and presented to the User, allowing for convenient evaluation and decision-making This endeavor could significantly improve timeliness, accuracy, and efficiency of information retrieval for end-Users… …Leading to enhanced decision-making, job performance, and ultimately, increased combat readiness Introduction [SLIDE 26]: 26 Hypothesis: AI LLMs do transform training and performance support by enabling a custom query capability, within an Embedded Help/Embedded Training (EH/ET) application, to elevate User learning, information retrieval accuracy and efficiency, improved job performance and thereby enhance combat readiness Perceived Outcomes: Use of AI LLM to design, develop, integrate, and test prototype EH/ET software with AI-generated/displayed User content Use of AI LLM to demonstrate prototype to swiftly retrieve User-queried/prompted information/results Document specific processes, procedures, and software required to design, develop, integrate, and test EH/ET prototype, including library of applicable prompts Hypothesis [SLIDE 27]: 27 Tracking Generative AI Requirements 27 [SLIDE 28]: 28 Infrastructure/Tools [SLIDE 29]: 29 Infrastructure/Tools, continued [SLIDE 30]: 30 We can upload documents into pSDO PrivateGPT source documents file Can also delete previously uploaded documents Each time PrivateGPT is indexed, it will check source documents folder and ingest what is there and/or delete what is not there Private GPT is CPU based; response times are longer than GPU based models Average response has taken ~300ms We indexed 7000+ pages of Public Domain Army Doctrinal Publications in ~ 30 mins Model selection is currently optimized for privacy (read DoD Cyber Compliance) not performance We used the MCCC Study Guide to query our curated data set consisting of over 7000 pages of Army Doctrine We specifically developed 3 specific and unique queries Model provided doctrinally correct responses to all three This capability has great potential and will improve significantly as we continue to train and develop prompts and engineer the model Observations/Lessons Learned [SLIDE 31]: 31 Reviewed/Analyzed 18 LLMs 17 deemed Non-Cyber compliant due to interaction with www Currently programs. These partners are true collaborators through joint investment that have strong technical capabilities underscored with a well-established relationship with Leidos. This is the starting point for most companies and are partners who have the capabilities that work well within Leidos’s framework and are willing to develop an initial relationship with Leidos.
